5 Differential Expression Calculation (limma-trend, limma-voom, dream)

Published

2026-07-23

Differential expression analysis (5)

Protocol reader guidance

This notebook is written for users applying the workflow to their own dataset. Read each section in order, confirm required inputs before running code sections, and use the YAML parameters at the top of the file to adapt paths and thresholds for your study design.

Before you run

Validate input data and metadata column names first. Most runtime issues in protocol use are caused by path mismatches, missing files, or dataset-specific column naming differences.

Path portability check

The setup section checks the full input and output path lengths against portable_path_limit before analysis begins. Generated diagnostic filenames are shortened in a fixed, repeatable way when needed, and the fixed filenames that later stages must read are never renamed. Fix any path flagged as too long before continuing.

Reproducibility caution

When editing parameters, record exactly what changed (paths, thresholds, model options) in your run notes so later figures and conclusions can be reproduced during review and publication.

This notebook is the calculation-only component of the DE workflow. It performs model fitting, exports contrast-level result tables/statistics, and writes result files read by the companion visualisation notebook (6_Differential_Expression_Visualisation.qmd).

The protocol-supported method set in this stage is: limma-trend, limma-voom, and dream. These are the only DE engines treated as publication-path defaults in this notebook.

YAML parameter order guide (5)

To make adaptation easier for new users, parameters are grouped in execution order:

  1. Project paths and shared folders.
  2. Input locations from earlier stages.
  3. Stage output folders.
  4. Statistical thresholds and contrast controls.
  5. Metadata mappings (col_* values for your dataset).
  6. Method/enrichment options.
  7. Supported model settings.
  8. Runtime/cache controls.

Supported DE methods in this protocol

  • limma-trend (default linear model with empirical Bayes moderation).
  • limma-voom (for count-like inputs where mean-variance modelling is desirable).
  • dream (mixed-model option retained for richer random-effect modelling output).
Model-input discipline

Each DE branch uses an explicit assay and transformation before model fitting. limma-trend_Q3 uses the stage 3 Q3 assay after log2 transformation with a small prior count, limma-trend_GeoDiff uses the GeoDiff normmat assay directly, and voom branches use count-like matrices with TMM scaling factors. Review the model input check later in this notebook before interpreting any DE result.

Why the GeoDiff limma inputs differ

Both methods use limma, but they are not given the same input.

limma-voom works from count-like data so it can estimate mean-variance weights before model fitting. Therefore, limma_voom_GeoDiff uses the GeoDiff-filtered count object (GeoDiff_spatial_data.RDS).

limma-trend is used with data that has already been normalized and put on a log-like scale. For that reason, limma_trend_GeoDiff uses the GeoDiff-normalized normmat data from GeoDiff_normalised_spatial_data.RDS.

Supported model choices

  • This notebook supports random-intercept models as the default mixed-model design.
  • The random effect is controlled by params$random_effect_var and can be left blank.
  • If your design needs nested/random slopes or multiple high-dimensional random effects, this notebook intentionally does not support that.

When to keep the model simple: If you have limited RAM/CPU or many contrasts with many samples, prefer limma-trend first. Keep dream for targeted follow-up where richer mixed-model output is essential.

What runs in this notebook (and what does not)

  • Runs in stage 5 (calculation): model fitting, contrast-level DE statistics, DE gene extraction, diagnostics, and structured result-file export.
  • Does not run in stage 5: interpretation figures (volcano panels, heatmaps, correlation plots, enrichment comparison plots).
  • Where figures are generated: 6_Differential_Expression_Visualisation.qmd.

Model specification options

By default, DE models use the generated Annotation group as the contrast factor, but this is a fallback rather than a requirement. Set params$contrast_factor to any metadata column that should define the biological groups being compared. Internally the selected factor is copied to DE_Group, so limma and dream use one consistent model column across all normalization objects. You can optionally add adjustment covariates via typed YAML lists:

  • params$model_covariates_continuous for numeric covariates such as age.
  • params$model_covariates_categorical for factor covariates such as sex, slide_name, or batch.
Separate cell_type and disease_status columns are not required

The fallback Annotation group (composed as <cell_type>_<disease_status>) is built and used only when params$contrast_factor is left at its default value "Annotation" — this is the exemplar configuration for the bundled dataset. If you set contrast_factor to any existing metadata column (for example a single combined segment/condition column), that column defines the comparison directly and the separate col_cell_type / col_disease_status columns are optional: where they are absent the notebook falls back to sensible placeholders and continues. In other words, your project does not need to carry distinct cell-type and segment columns to use this workflow.

Prefer planned pairwise contrasts for primary comparisons

Use model_mode: "pairwise_mixed" with an explicit contrast file for planned biological comparisons whenever the study question is naturally pairwise. The one_vs_rest_mixed mode is useful for broad screening and for the exemplar dataset because it can automatically generate contrasts, but it is usually not the best primary inferential choice.

By averaging diverse groups into a single baseline, one-vs-rest contrasts can mask true differences and allow a single outlier group to skew the result. If a one-vs-rest screen identifies a signal, follow up with biologically meaningful pairwise contrasts before carrying results into interpretation or enrichment.

Covariate information

When covariates are provided, this notebook validates all requested columns before model fitting. Execution stops with a clear error if any covariate is missing, contains missing values, has only one observed value after filtering, or cannot be encoded as the requested type.

Continuous covariates are modeled as numeric terms. Before Stage 5, confirm that columns such as age are numeric in the metadata produced by the earlier stages. If an age column contains text values or mixed encodings, correct the metadata in an earlier stage and rerun the staged workflow so the numeric value entering Stage 5 is reproducible.

Common configuration examples

Default model (recommended starting point):

model_covariates_continuous:
  value: []
model_covariates_categorical:
  value: []

Add biological/technical adjustment terms:

model_covariates_continuous:
  value: ["age"]
model_covariates_categorical:
  value: ["sex", "slide_name"]

These covariates are added to the fixed-effect model formula for limma-trend, limma-voom, and dream while preserving the selected contrast factor and the one-vs-rest / pairwise contrast system.

How to interpret this stage

Use this stage to make explicit, reproducible decisions before progressing:

  1. Confirm the key output objects/tables were produced without unexpected warnings.
  2. Review the stage figures in the context of your study design (technical quality, group balance, and model assumptions).
  3. Record your decision in run notes (for example: “acceptable”, “rerun with adjusted parameters”, or “exclude affected samples/groups”) before moving to the next notebook.
Abbreviation glossary used across stages 5 and 6

This glossary is included so users can interpret model outputs and later figures without prior project context.

  • DE: Differential expression.
  • FC: Fold change (effect size).
  • FDR: False discovery rate (multiple-testing adjusted p-value).
  • Q3: Third-quartile normalisation.
  • TMM: Trimmed mean of M-values normalisation.

Load libraries

Show code 
# startup package warnings are expected and do not affect protocol runs.
#| warning: false
#| message: false

library(NanoStringNCTools)
library(GeomxTools)
library(GeoMxWorkflows)
library(tidyverse)
library(factoextra)
library(reshape2)
library(pheatmap)
library(writexl)
library(ggrepel)
library(edgeR)
library(limma)
library(statmod)
library(variancePartition)
library(janitor)
library(VennDiagram)
library(cowplot)
library(doParallel)
library(lme4)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(wesanderson)
library(randomcoloR)
library(SummarizedExperiment)
library(fs)
library(clusterProfiler)

Setup paths and load stage inputs

Show code 
# path setup can emit benign environment warnings; suppress by protocol design.

root <- params$project_root
if (is.null(root) || root == "" || root == ".") {
  # YAML-aware fallback: use repository root discovered via here().
  root <- path_abs(here::here())
} else {
  root <- path_abs(root)
}

# here()-first helper with explicit YAML-root override when provided.
root_via_here <- path_abs(here::here())
use_here_root <- identical(path_abs(root), root_via_here)
project_path <- function(...) {
  if (use_here_root) {
    here::here(...)
  } else {
    path(root, ...)
  }
}

# Resolve shared source directory from the project-level src folder.
dir_src <- project_path(params$dir_src)
dir_data <- project_path(params$dir_data)
path_utilities_path <- path(dir_src, "pathUtilities.R")
if (!file_exists(path_utilities_path)) {
  stop("Required path helper not found: ", path_utilities_path, call. = FALSE)
}
source(path_utilities_path)
portable_path_limit <- protocol_validate_path_limit(params$portable_path_limit)

geodiff_input_path <- project_path(params$dir_outputs, params$geodiff_stage, params$geodiff_results_dir, params$geodiff_rds)
norm_input_path <- project_path(params$dir_outputs, params$norm_stage, params$norm_results_dir, params$norm_rds)
norm_geodiff_input_path <- project_path(params$dir_outputs, params$norm_stage, params$norm_results_dir, params$norm_geodiff_rds)
norm_tmm_input_path <- project_path(params$dir_outputs, params$norm_stage, params$norm_results_dir, params$norm_tmm_rds)

# Contrast file resolution. Contrast tables are user analysis inputs, so the
# default lookup location is Data/ rather than src/.
contrast_file_path <- NA_character_
if (!is.null(params$contrast_file) && nzchar(params$contrast_file)) {
  contrast_file_candidates <- c(
    path(dir_data, params$contrast_file),
    project_path(params$contrast_file)
  )
  contrast_file_path <- contrast_file_candidates[file_exists(contrast_file_candidates)][1]
  if (is.na(contrast_file_path) && identical(params$model_mode, "pairwise_mixed")) {
    stop(
      "Contrast file not found. Checked: ",
      paste(contrast_file_candidates, collapse = " ; "),
      ". Place the file in Data/ and set params$contrast_file to its filename, or provide an explicit project-relative path.",
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }
} else if (identical(params$model_mode, "pairwise_mixed")) {
  stop(
    "params$model_mode is 'pairwise_mixed', but params$contrast_file is blank. ",
    "Place a contrast file in Data/ and set params$contrast_file to the filename.",
    call. = FALSE
  )
}

out_base <- project_path(params$dir_outputs, params$out_stage)
out_results <- path(out_base, params$out_results)
out_tables <- path(out_base, params$out_tables)
out_figures <- path(out_base, params$out_figures)

out_results_de <- path(out_results, params$de_results_subdir)
out_figures_de <- path(out_figures, params$de_figures_subdir)
legacy_out_results_de <- path(out_results, "differential_expression")

stage5_contract_paths <- c(
  protocol_contract_path(out_results_de, "dream_results.RDS", portable_path_limit, "Stage 5 dream result"),
  protocol_contract_path(out_results_de, "limma_voom_GeoDiff_results.RDS", portable_path_limit, "Stage 5 limma-voom result"),
  protocol_contract_path(out_results_de, "limma_trend_GeoDiff_results.RDS", portable_path_limit, "Stage 5 limma-trend result")
)
stage5_path_audit <- protocol_path_audit(
  c(
    "GeoDiff input" = geodiff_input_path,
    "Q3 normalized input" = norm_input_path,
    "TMM normalized input" = norm_tmm_input_path,
    stats::setNames(stage5_contract_paths, paste0("DE contract ", seq_along(stage5_contract_paths))),
    "DE figure directory" = out_figures_de
  ),
  portable_path_limit
)
protocol_assert_audit(stage5_path_audit, portable_path_limit)
knitr::kable(stage5_path_audit, caption = "Stage 5 path-portability audit")
Stage 5 path-portability audit
path_role resolved_path path_characters remaining_characters status
GeoDiff input C:/R/gmx/Example_Dataset/Outputs/2_GeoDiff/results/GeoDiff_spatial_data.RDS 75 125 Portable
Q3 normalized input C:/R/gmx/Example_Dataset/Outputs/3_Norm/results/normalised_spatial_data.RDS 75 125 Portable
TMM normalized input C:/R/gmx/Example_Dataset/Outputs/3_Norm/results/TMM_normalised_spatial_data.RDS 79 121 Portable
DE contract 1 C:/R/gmx/Example_Dataset/Outputs/5_DE/results/de/dream_results.RDS 66 134 Portable
DE contract 2 C:/R/gmx/Example_Dataset/Outputs/5_DE/results/de/limma_voom_GeoDiff_results.RDS 79 121 Portable
DE contract 3 C:/R/gmx/Example_Dataset/Outputs/5_DE/results/de/limma_trend_GeoDiff_results.RDS 80 120 Portable
DE figure directory C:/R/gmx/Example_Dataset/Outputs/5_DE/figures/de 48 152 Portable
Show code 
walk(list(out_results_de, out_tables, out_figures_de), dir_create, recurse = TRUE)

# Preserve reuse of existing Stage 5 caches without continuing to write to the
# legacy long directory.
legacy_cache_names <- c(
  "dream_results.RDS",
  "limma_voom_TMM_results.RDS",
  "limma_voom_GeoDiff_results.RDS",
  "limma_trend_Q3_results.RDS",
  "limma_trend_GeoDiff_results.RDS"
)
cache_reuse_requested <- any(c(
  isTRUE(params$use_dream_cache),
  isTRUE(params$use_limma_voom_cache),
  isTRUE(params$use_limma_trend_cache)
))
if (cache_reuse_requested && dir_exists(legacy_out_results_de)) {
  for (cache_name in legacy_cache_names) {
    compact_cache <- path(out_results_de, cache_name)
    legacy_cache <- path(legacy_out_results_de, cache_name)
    if (!file_exists(compact_cache) && file_exists(legacy_cache)) {
      file.copy(legacy_cache, compact_cache, overwrite = FALSE)
      message("Copied legacy Stage 5 cache into the compact output directory: ", cache_name)
    }
  }
}

# Suppress package-version warnings from shared helper sourcing
# so the setup section remains focused on path and input validation.
utility_functions_path <- path(dir_src, "utilityFunctions.R")
if (!file_exists(utility_functions_path)) {
  stop(
    "Required helper file not found: ", utility_functions_path,
    ". Set params$project_root to the project root and params$dir_src to the helper directory name, usually 'src'.",
    call. = FALSE
  )
}
suppressPackageStartupMessages(suppressWarnings(source(utility_functions_path)))

Random-effect modeling diagnostic

This diagnostic records the random-intercept setting declared in the YAML before model fitting begins. Confirm that the selected column identifies the repeated or matched segment unit for your study design; if no column is declared, later-stage models run without random-intercept correction.

Random-effect configuration declared for this Stage 5 run.
Setting Value
YAML parameter grouping_id
Model behavior Use this metadata column for random-intercept correction where supported.
User check Confirm this column exists after metadata preparation and represents the intended repeated-measures unit.

The helper section below defines all reusable utilities for this notebook. These functions are intentionally grouped in one place so readers can inspect the full data-preparation and model-support logic before any DE model is run.

Show code 
# helper registration may trigger incidental package warnings not relevant to analysis logic.
#| warning: false

sanitize_col_name <- function(x, default_name) {
  if (is.null(x) || !nzchar(trimws(as.character(x)))) return(default_name)
  trimws(as.character(x))
}

col_slide <- sanitize_col_name(params$col_slide, "slide_name")
col_patient <- sanitize_col_name(params$col_patient, "patient_id")
col_disease <- sanitize_col_name(params$col_disease_status, "disease_status")
col_cell_type <- sanitize_col_name(params$col_cell_type, "cell_type")
col_segment <- sanitize_col_name(params$col_segment, "segment")
contrast_factor <- sanitize_col_name(params$contrast_factor, "Annotation")
model_group_col <- "DE_Group"

render_protocol_kable <- function(.data, caption, ...) {
  table_text <- suppressWarnings(knitr::kable(.data, caption = caption, ...))
  knitr::asis_output(paste(as.character(table_text), collapse = "\n"))
}

ensure_metadata_column <- function(pd, target_col, candidate_cols, default_value, label_text) {
  if (target_col %in% colnames(pd)) return(pd)

  candidate_hit <- candidate_cols[candidate_cols %in% colnames(pd)]
  if (length(candidate_hit) > 0) {
    pd[[target_col]] <- pd[[candidate_hit[1]]]
    warning(
      "Requested ", label_text, " column '", target_col, "' was not found. ",
      "Using fallback column '", candidate_hit[1], "' copied into '", target_col, "'."
    )
    return(pd)
  }

  pd[[target_col]] <- default_value
  warning(
    "Requested ", label_text, " column '", target_col, "' was not found and no fallback column matched. ",
    "Created placeholder column '", target_col, "' for protocol continuity."
  )
  pd
}

# Standardize disease labels so Annotation construction is stable across datasets.
normalize_disease_labels <- function(x) {
  x <- as.character(x)
  x <- stringr::str_trim(x)
  x_low <- stringr::str_to_lower(x)
  dplyr::case_when(
    x_low %in% c("healthy", "normal") ~ "Healthy",
    x_low %in% c("tumour", "tumor", "disease") ~ "Tumor",
    TRUE ~ stringr::str_to_title(x)
  )
}

# Build consistent sample metadata, create the fallback Annotation group, and
# Sanitise group labels for modeling with make.names(), but abort if two distinct
# input labels collapse to the same sanitised name. Without this, values such as
# "CD4+ T" and "CD4 T" both become "CD4..T" and are silently pooled into one DE
# group, corrupting the comparison.
make_group_names_checked <- function(x, what = "group label") {
  x <- as.character(x)
  sanitized <- make.names(x)
  collapsed <- tapply(x, sanitized, function(vals) length(unique(vals)) > 1)
  if (any(collapsed)) {
    bad <- names(collapsed)[collapsed]
    detail <- vapply(bad, function(nm) {
      paste0(nm, " <- {", paste(sort(unique(x[sanitized == nm])), collapse = ", "), "}")
    }, character(1))
    stop(
      "make.names() collapsed distinct ", what, " values into the same modeling name, ",
      "which would silently pool different groups:\n  ", paste(detail, collapse = "\n  "),
      "\nRename these values in the metadata so they stay distinct after sanitization.",
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }
  sanitized
}

# copy the configured contrast factor into DE_Group for modeling.
prepare_metadata <- function(obj, contrast_factor_col = contrast_factor, model_group = model_group_col) {
  pd <- get_pheno(obj)

  pd <- ensure_metadata_column(
    pd, col_slide, c("slide_name", "slide", "Slide"),
    default_value = "slide_1", label_text = "slide"
  )
  pd <- ensure_metadata_column(
    pd, col_patient, c("patient_id", "patient", "PatientID"),
    default_value = rownames(pd), label_text = "patient"
  )
  pd <- ensure_metadata_column(
    pd, col_disease, c("disease_status", "disease", "condition", "status"),
    default_value = "UnknownDisease", label_text = "disease status"
  )
  pd <- ensure_metadata_column(
    pd, col_cell_type, c("cell_type", "celltype", "annotation", "segment"),
    default_value = "UnknownCellType", label_text = "cell type"
  )
  pd <- ensure_metadata_column(
    pd, col_segment, c("segment", "annotation", "region"),
    default_value = rownames(pd), label_text = "segment"
  )

  pd[[col_disease]] <- normalize_disease_labels(pd[[col_disease]])
  pd$Annotation <- make_group_names_checked(
    paste0(pd[[col_cell_type]], "_", pd[[col_disease]]),
    "Annotation (cell type _ disease status)"
  )

  if (!(contrast_factor_col %in% colnames(pd))) {
    stop(
      sprintf(
        "contrast_factor '%s' was not found in metadata after fallback Annotation construction. Available metadata columns are: %s.",
        contrast_factor_col,
        paste(sort(colnames(pd)), collapse = ", ")
      ),
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }

  contrast_values <- trimws(as.character(pd[[contrast_factor_col]]))
  missing_contrast_values <- is.na(contrast_values) | contrast_values == ""
  if (any(missing_contrast_values)) {
    stop(
      sprintf(
        "contrast_factor '%s' contains %d missing or blank value(s). Fill or remove these samples before running DE.",
        contrast_factor_col,
        sum(missing_contrast_values)
      ),
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }

  pd[[model_group]] <- make_group_names_checked(
    contrast_values,
    paste0("contrast factor '", contrast_factor_col, "'")
  )

  set_pheno(obj, pd)
}

# Optional helper to drop low-power DE groups from an object.
remove_low_power_groups <- function(obj, low_power_groups = character(), group_col = model_group_col) {
  if (length(low_power_groups) == 0) return(obj)
  pd <- get_pheno(obj)
  if (!(group_col %in% colnames(pd))) {
    stop("Cannot remove low-power groups because modeling column is missing: ", group_col, call. = FALSE)
  }
  bad <- make.names(as.character(low_power_groups))
  keep <- !(pd[[group_col]] %in% bad)
  if (any(!keep)) {
    message("Dropping low-power DE group(s): ", paste(sort(unique(pd[[group_col]][!keep])), collapse = ", "))
  }
  subset_samples(obj, keep)
}

eligible_group_levels <- function(pd, group_col = model_group_col, min_group_n = 2) {
  if (!(group_col %in% colnames(pd))) {
    stop("Cannot determine eligible DE groups because modeling column is missing: ", group_col, call. = FALSE)
  }
  pd2 <- as.data.frame(pd)
  pd2[[group_col]] <- make.names(as.character(pd2[[group_col]]))
  pd2 %>%
    dplyr::count(.data[[group_col]], name = "n") %>%
    dplyr::filter(n >= min_group_n) %>%
    dplyr::pull(.data[[group_col]]) %>%
    unique() %>%
    sort()
}


# Generate a transparent diagnostics table that flags low-power groups.
# This keeps exclusion logic user-controlled via YAML rather than hard-coded.
find_low_power_groups <- function(pd, group_col = model_group_col, min_samples_per_group = 2, min_patients_per_group = "", patient_col = col_patient) {
  if (!(group_col %in% colnames(pd))) {
    stop("Cannot run low-power diagnostics because modeling column is missing: ", group_col, call. = FALSE)
  }
  pd2 <- pd %>% dplyr::mutate(.de_group = .data[[group_col]])
  group_counts <- pd2 %>% dplyr::count(.de_group, name = "n_samples")
  group_patient_counts <- pd2 %>% dplyr::group_by(.de_group) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(n_patients = dplyr::n_distinct(.data[[patient_col]]), .groups = "drop")

  diag_tbl <- dplyr::left_join(group_counts, group_patient_counts, by = ".de_group") %>%
    dplyr::rename(!!group_col := .de_group)
  diag_tbl$low_sample_power <- diag_tbl$n_samples < min_samples_per_group

  use_patient_rule <- !is.null(min_patients_per_group) && nzchar(as.character(min_patients_per_group))
  if (use_patient_rule) {
    min_pt <- as.numeric(min_patients_per_group)
    diag_tbl$low_patient_power <- diag_tbl$n_patients < min_pt
  } else {
    diag_tbl$low_patient_power <- FALSE
  }

  diag_tbl$low_power <- diag_tbl$low_sample_power | diag_tbl$low_patient_power
  diag_tbl
}

# Generic metadata getter so later code works with multiple object classes.
get_pheno <- function(obj) {
  # Normalisation outputs from earlier stages are not always the same class.
  # This helper standardises metadata access across ExpressionSet,
  # SummarizedExperiment, and edgeR::DGEList objects.
  if (inherits(obj, "DGEList")) {
    return(as.data.frame(obj$samples))
  }
  if (methods::is(obj, "ExpressionSet")) {
    return(Biobase::pData(obj))
  }
  if (methods::is(obj, "SummarizedExperiment")) {
    return(as.data.frame(SummarizedExperiment::colData(obj)))
  }
  stop("Unsupported object type for metadata extraction: ", paste(class(obj), collapse = ", "), call. = FALSE)
}

# Generic metadata setter matching the object-class dispatch above.
set_pheno <- function(obj, pd) {
  if (inherits(obj, "DGEList")) {
    obj$samples <- as.data.frame(pd)
    return(obj)
  }
  if (methods::is(obj, "ExpressionSet")) {
    Biobase::pData(obj) <- pd
    return(obj)
  }
  if (methods::is(obj, "SummarizedExperiment")) {
    SummarizedExperiment::colData(obj) <- S4Vectors::DataFrame(pd)
    return(obj)
  }
  stop("Unsupported object type for metadata assignment: ", paste(class(obj), collapse = ", "), call. = FALSE)
}

# Generic expression matrix getter used by all DE methods and plotting blocks.
get_expr_mat <- function(obj) {
  if (inherits(obj, "DGEList")) {
    return(as.matrix(obj$counts))
  }
  if (methods::is(obj, "ExpressionSet")) {
    return(Biobase::exprs(obj))
  }
  if (methods::is(obj, "SummarizedExperiment")) {
    return(as.matrix(SummarizedExperiment::assay(obj)))
  }
  stop("Unsupported object type for expression extraction: ", paste(class(obj), collapse = ", "), call. = FALSE)
}

# Explicit assay getter for model branches. This is stricter than get_expr_mat()
# so branch names cannot silently fall back to raw exprs when a normalised assay
# was requested.
get_assay_matrix <- function(obj, assay_name = "exprs", context = "model input") {
  assay_name <- as.character(assay_name[[1]])

  if (inherits(obj, "DGEList")) {
    if (assay_name %in% c("counts", "exprs", "raw_counts")) {
      return(as.matrix(obj$counts))
    }
    stop(
      "Assay '", assay_name, "' is not available for DGEList input in ", context,
      ". Use 'counts' for DGEList objects.",
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }

  if (methods::is(obj, "ExpressionSet")) {
    assay_names <- Biobase::assayDataElementNames(obj)
    if (!(assay_name %in% assay_names)) {
      stop(
        "Assay '", assay_name, "' was not found in ", context,
        ". Available assay elements are: ", paste(assay_names, collapse = ", "),
        call. = FALSE
      )
    }
    return(as.matrix(Biobase::assayDataElement(obj, assay_name)))
  }

  if (methods::is(obj, "SummarizedExperiment")) {
    assay_names <- SummarizedExperiment::assayNames(obj)
    if (length(assay_names) == 0 && assay_name %in% c("assay", "exprs")) {
      return(as.matrix(SummarizedExperiment::assay(obj)))
    }
    if (!(assay_name %in% assay_names)) {
      stop(
        "Assay '", assay_name, "' was not found in ", context,
        ". Available assay names are: ", paste(assay_names, collapse = ", "),
        call. = FALSE
      )
    }
    return(as.matrix(SummarizedExperiment::assay(obj, assay_name)))
  }

  stop("Unsupported object type for assay extraction: ", paste(class(obj), collapse = ", "), call. = FALSE)
}

matrix_is_integer_like <- function(mat, tolerance = sqrt(.Machine$double.eps)) {
  finite_values <- as.numeric(mat[is.finite(mat)])
  if (length(finite_values) == 0) return(NA)
  all(abs(finite_values - round(finite_values)) <= tolerance)
}

drop_rows_with_na <- function(mat, context = "model input") {
  mat <- as.matrix(mat)
  keep <- rowSums(is.na(mat)) == 0
  dropped <- sum(!keep)
  if (dropped > 0) {
    message("Dropping ", dropped, " rows with NA values from ", context, ".")
  }
  mat <- mat[keep, , drop = FALSE]
  if (nrow(mat) < 2 || ncol(mat) < 2) {
    stop("Not enough data remaining in ", context, " after removing NA rows.", call. = FALSE)
  }
  mat
}

apply_model_input_transform <- function(mat, transform = "identity", prior_count = 0, context = "model input") {
  mat <- as.matrix(mat)

  if (identical(transform, "identity")) {
    return(mat)
  }

  if (identical(transform, "log2_plus_prior")) {
    prior_count <- as.numeric(prior_count)
    if (length(prior_count) != 1 || is.na(prior_count) || prior_count < 0) {
      stop("limma_trend_prior_count must be a single non-negative number.", call. = FALSE)
    }
    if (any((mat + prior_count) <= 0, na.rm = TRUE)) {
      stop(
        "Cannot log2-transform ", context,
        " because at least one value plus prior_count is <= 0.",
        call. = FALSE
      )
    }
    return(log2(mat + prior_count))
  }

  stop("Unsupported model input transform: ", transform, call. = FALSE)
}

prepare_model_expression_matrix <- function(
  obj,
  assay_name,
  transform = "identity",
  prior_count = 0,
  context = "model input",
  clean_na = TRUE
) {
  mat <- get_assay_matrix(obj, assay_name = assay_name, context = context)
  mat <- apply_model_input_transform(mat, transform = transform, prior_count = prior_count, context = context)
  if (isTRUE(clean_na)) {
    mat <- drop_rows_with_na(mat, context = context)
  }
  mat
}

# Remove rows containing NA values and validate count-like input before voom/dream.
clean_count_matrix <- function(mat, context = "count matrix") {
  mat <- drop_rows_with_na(mat, context = context)
  finite_values <- as.numeric(mat[is.finite(mat)])

  if (any(finite_values < 0)) {
    stop("Negative values found in ", context, "; voom/dream count branches require count-like non-negative input.", call. = FALSE)
  }

  if (!isTRUE(matrix_is_integer_like(mat))) {
    warning(
      context,
      " is not integer-like. Confirm that a count-like assay, not a transformed normalised assay, was supplied to the voom/dream branch."
    )
  }

  mat
}

# Resolve user-requested random-effect variable; warn and fall back to fixed-effects when absent.
resolve_random_effect_var <- function(pd, requested_var) {
  if (!is.null(requested_var) && nzchar(requested_var) && requested_var %in% colnames(pd)) {
    return(requested_var)
  }
  if (!is.null(requested_var) && nzchar(requested_var) && !(requested_var %in% colnames(pd))) {
    warning("random_effect_var was provided but not found in metadata: ", requested_var, ". Running without random intercept.")
  }
  NA_character_
}

normalize_model_covariates <- function(x) {
  if (is.null(x) || length(x) == 0) return(character(0))
  out <- as.character(unlist(x, use.names = FALSE))
  out <- trimws(out)
  out <- out[nzchar(out)]
  unique(out)
}

resolve_model_covariate_spec <- function(continuous = character(0), categorical = character(0)) {
  continuous <- normalize_model_covariates(continuous)
  categorical <- normalize_model_covariates(categorical)

  duplicated_terms <- intersect(continuous, categorical)
  if (length(duplicated_terms) > 0) {
    stop(
      "The same covariate cannot be listed as both continuous and categorical: ",
      paste(duplicated_terms, collapse = ", "),
      ". Choose the intended model encoding and keep each covariate in one YAML list.",
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }

  list(
    continuous = continuous,
    categorical = categorical
  )
}

model_covariate_names <- function(covariate_spec) {
  unique(c(covariate_spec$continuous, covariate_spec$categorical))
}

format_covariate_list <- function(x) {
  if (length(x) == 0) "None" else paste(x, collapse = ", ")
}

quote_formula_term <- function(x) {
  x <- as.character(x[[1]])
  if (identical(make.names(x), x)) return(x)
  paste0("`", gsub("`", "\\\\`", x), "`")
}

build_fixed_effect_formula_text <- function(covariate_spec, group_col = model_group_col) {
  rhs_terms <- c(group_col, model_covariate_names(covariate_spec))
  paste("~ 0 +", paste(vapply(rhs_terms, quote_formula_term, character(1)), collapse = " + "))
}

coerce_continuous_covariate <- function(x, covariate_name) {
  raw_chr <- trimws(as.character(x))
  raw_missing <- is.na(x) | is.na(raw_chr) | !nzchar(raw_chr)

  if (is.numeric(x) || is.integer(x)) {
    out <- as.numeric(x)
  } else {
    out <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(raw_chr))
    failed_parse <- !raw_missing & is.na(out)
    if (any(failed_parse)) {
      examples <- unique(raw_chr[failed_parse])
      examples <- examples[!is.na(examples)]
      stop(
        "Continuous covariate '", covariate_name, "' could not be parsed as numeric. ",
        "Example non-numeric value(s): ", paste(utils::head(examples, 5), collapse = ", "), ". ",
        "Clean this metadata column in an earlier stage before running Stage 5, or move the covariate to ",
        "model_covariates_categorical only if categorical modeling is scientifically intended.",
        call. = FALSE
      )
    }
    message(sprintf("Parsed continuous covariate '%s' as numeric for model fitting.", covariate_name))
  }

  out[raw_missing] <- NA_real_
  out
}

coerce_categorical_covariate <- function(x, covariate_name) {
  out <- trimws(as.character(x))
  out[is.na(x) | is.na(out) | !nzchar(out)] <- NA_character_
  message(sprintf("Encoding categorical covariate '%s' as a factor for model fitting.", covariate_name))
  factor(out)
}

prepare_model_metadata <- function(pd, covariate_spec) {
  covariates <- model_covariate_names(covariate_spec)
  missing_cov <- setdiff(covariates, colnames(pd))
  if (length(missing_cov) > 0) {
    stop(
      sprintf(
        "Requested model covariates not found in metadata: %s.",
        paste(missing_cov, collapse = ", ")
      ),
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }

  for (cov in covariate_spec$continuous) {
    pd[[cov]] <- coerce_continuous_covariate(pd[[cov]], cov)
  }
  for (cov in covariate_spec$categorical) {
    pd[[cov]] <- coerce_categorical_covariate(pd[[cov]], cov)
  }

  pd
}

validate_model_covariates <- function(pd, covariate_spec) {
  covariates <- model_covariate_names(covariate_spec)
  if (length(covariates) == 0) {
    message(sprintf("Model formula mode: default (~0 + %s). No additional fixed-effect covariates requested.", model_group_col))
    return(invisible(pd))
  }

  missing_cov <- setdiff(covariates, colnames(pd))
  if (length(missing_cov) > 0) {
    stop(
      sprintf(
        "Requested model covariates not found in metadata: %s.",
        paste(missing_cov, collapse = ", ")
      ),
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }

  missing_value_counts <- vapply(covariates, function(cn) sum(is.na(pd[[cn]])), integer(1))
  covariates_with_missing_values <- names(missing_value_counts)[missing_value_counts > 0]
  if (length(covariates_with_missing_values) > 0) {
    stop(
      sprintf(
        "Requested model covariates contain missing values after type preparation: %s. Remove, impute, or correct these values in an earlier stage before Stage 5.",
        paste(
          sprintf("%s (%d missing)", covariates_with_missing_values, missing_value_counts[covariates_with_missing_values]),
          collapse = ", "
        )
      ),
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }

  nonfinite_continuous <- covariate_spec$continuous[vapply(covariate_spec$continuous, function(cn) {
    any(!is.finite(pd[[cn]]))
  }, logical(1))]
  if (length(nonfinite_continuous) > 0) {
    stop(
      "Continuous model covariates contain non-finite values after numeric conversion: ",
      paste(nonfinite_continuous, collapse = ", "),
      ". Correct these metadata values in an earlier stage before Stage 5.",
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }

  bad_cov <- covariates[vapply(covariates, function(cn) {
    n_distinct(stats::na.omit(pd[[cn]])) < 2
  }, logical(1))]
  if (length(bad_cov) > 0) {
    stop(
      sprintf(
        "Requested model covariates with <2 observed values after type preparation: %s. Remove these covariates or verify metadata values.",
        paste(bad_cov, collapse = ", ")
      ),
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }

  message(sprintf("Model formula mode: ~0 + %s + %s", model_group_col, paste(covariates, collapse = " + ")))
  invisible(pd)
}

validate_design_matrix <- function(design, context = "model design") {
  design_rank <- qr(design)$rank
  if (design_rank < ncol(design)) {
    stop(
      "The ", context, " is not full rank (rank ", design_rank, " for ",
      ncol(design), " columns). This usually means one or more covariates are ",
      "redundant with the contrast groups or with each other. Remove or recode ",
      "the redundant covariate before interpreting DE results.",
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }
  invisible(TRUE)
}

build_limma_design <- function(pd, covariate_spec, group_col = model_group_col) {
  if (!(group_col %in% colnames(pd))) {
    stop("Model group column not found in metadata: ", group_col, call. = FALSE)
  }
  form_text <- build_fixed_effect_formula_text(covariate_spec, group_col = group_col)
  design <- model.matrix(stats::as.formula(form_text), data = pd)
  if (nrow(design) != nrow(pd)) {
    stop(
      "The limma model matrix has fewer rows than the prepared metadata. ",
      "Check for missing values in model covariates or the contrast factor before fitting.",
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }
  colnames(design) <- make.names(colnames(design))
  validate_design_matrix(design, context = "limma fixed-effect design")

  # Match group coefficient names to the contrast syntax configured by the user.
  #
  # Why this matters for protocol users:
  # - Contrast expressions are generated from selected DE group labels such as
  #   "Acinar_Healthy" or "control_duct".
  # - model.matrix(~ 0 + DE_Group) prefixes coefficient names with "DE_Group"
  #   (for example "DE_GroupAcinar_Healthy"), which is difficult for users to
  #   anticipate when writing or reviewing contrasts.
  #
  # We remove only the leading modeling-column token from group terms so
  # contrast expressions remain readable and directly interpretable by users.
  # Covariate columns (age, sex, slide_name, etc.) are left unchanged.
  group_prefix <- paste0("^", make.names(group_col))
  group_cols <- grepl(group_prefix, colnames(design))
  if (any(group_cols)) {
    colnames(design)[group_cols] <- sub(group_prefix, "", colnames(design)[group_cols])
  }

  list(design = design, formula_text = form_text)
}

# Validate that all symbols referenced inside contrast expressions are present
# in the fitted model coefficient names.
#
# This guard provides an explicit, user-friendly error before limma internals
# fail, and it prints both missing symbols and available coefficients so users
# can review dataset-specific naming issues.
validate_contrast_symbols <- function(contrast_expr, available_levels, context = "limma") {
  if (length(contrast_expr) == 0) return(invisible(TRUE))

  expr_text <- paste(contrast_expr, collapse = " ")
  tokens <- stringr::str_extract_all(expr_text, "[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_.]*")[[1]]
  tokens <- unique(tokens)

  # Exclude reserved math tokens and helper words that may appear in text.
  reserved <- c("Non")
  symbols <- setdiff(tokens, reserved)

  missing <- setdiff(symbols, available_levels)
  if (length(missing) > 0) {
    stop(
      paste0(
        "Contrast/Design naming mismatch detected in ", context, ". ",
        "The following contrast symbol(s) were not found in model coefficients: ",
        paste(missing, collapse = ", "), ". ",
        "Available coefficient names are: ",
        paste(sort(unique(available_levels)), collapse = ", "), ". ",
        "Please check params$contrast_factor, the contrast file, and contrast generation settings."
      ),
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }

  invisible(TRUE)
}


# Build a composite grouping ID (e.g., patient_id + location) for repeated-measures random effects.
add_grouping_id_column <- function(obj, grouping_id_vars, new_col = "grouping_id") {
  if (is.null(grouping_id_vars) || length(grouping_id_vars) == 0) return(obj)
  pd <- get_pheno(obj)
  missing_cols <- setdiff(grouping_id_vars, colnames(pd))
  if (length(missing_cols) > 0) {
    warning("Skipping grouping_id construction; missing column(s): ", paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", "))
    return(obj)
  }
  pd[[new_col]] <- apply(pd[, grouping_id_vars, drop = FALSE], 1, paste, collapse = "_")
  set_pheno(obj, pd)
}


subset_samples <- function(obj, keep) {
  if (inherits(obj, "DGEList")) {
    return(obj[, keep, keep.lib.sizes = FALSE])
  }
  if (methods::is(obj, "ExpressionSet") || methods::is(obj, "SummarizedExperiment")) {
    return(obj[, keep])
  }
  stop("Unsupported object type for sample subsetting: ", paste(class(obj), collapse = ", "), call. = FALSE)
}

# Parse and validate contrast specifications from YAML/file settings.
# Supports auto one-vs-rest generation and explicit pairwise definitions.
format_available_levels <- function(levels) {
  paste(sort(unique(levels)), collapse = ", ")
}

validate_contrast_level <- function(value, level_type, available_levels, contrast_factor_col) {
  raw_value <- trimws(as.character(value))
  clean_value <- make.names(raw_value)
  if (clean_value %in% available_levels) return(clean_value)

  sanitized_note <- if (!identical(raw_value, clean_value)) {
    sprintf(" (sanitized as '%s')", clean_value)
  } else {
    ""
  }

  stop(
    sprintf(
      "%s '%s'%s not found in contrast_factor '%s'. Available levels are: %s.",
      level_type,
      raw_value,
      sanitized_note,
      contrast_factor_col,
      format_available_levels(available_levels)
    ),
    call. = FALSE
  )
}

read_contrast_spec <- function(path_to_file, group_levels, model_mode = "one_vs_rest_mixed", contrast_factor_col = contrast_factor) {
  levs <- sort(unique(make.names(as.character(group_levels))))

  if (identical(model_mode, "one_vs_rest_mixed") && (is.na(path_to_file) || !nzchar(path_to_file))) {
    return(tibble::tibble(
      factor = contrast_factor_col,
      group_1 = levs,
      group_2 = paste0("Non", levs),
      contrast_expr = purrr::map_chr(levs, ~ paste0(
        .x, " - (", paste0(setdiff(levs, .x), collapse = " + "), ")/", length(setdiff(levs, .x))
      ))
    ))
  }

  if (is.na(path_to_file) || !nzchar(path_to_file) || !file_exists(path_to_file)) {
    stop("Contrast file not found: ", path_to_file, call. = FALSE)
  }

  spec <- tryCatch(readr::read_tsv(path_to_file, comment = "#", show_col_types = FALSE), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(spec) || ncol(spec) < 3 || !all(c("factor", "numerator", "denominator") %in% janitor::make_clean_names(colnames(spec)))) {
    warning("Contrast file could not be parsed as TSV; retrying as CSV.")
    spec <- readr::read_csv(path_to_file, comment = "#", show_col_types = FALSE)
  }

  spec <- spec %>% janitor::clean_names()
  required <- c("factor", "numerator", "denominator")
  missing <- setdiff(required, colnames(spec))
  if (length(missing) > 0) {
    stop("Contrast file must contain columns: factor, numerator, denominator. Missing: ", paste(missing, collapse = ", "), call. = FALSE)
  }

  spec$factor <- trimws(as.character(spec$factor))
  invalid_factors <- setdiff(unique(spec$factor), contrast_factor_col)
  if (length(invalid_factors) > 0) {
    stop(
      sprintf(
        "Contrast file factor values must match params$contrast_factor ('%s'). Found unsupported factor value(s): %s.",
        contrast_factor_col,
        paste(invalid_factors, collapse = ", ")
      ),
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }

  if (identical(model_mode, "one_vs_rest_mixed")) {
    rows <- purrr::pmap_dfr(spec, function(factor, numerator, denominator) {
      numerator <- validate_contrast_level(numerator, "Numerator", levs, contrast_factor_col)
      others <- setdiff(levs, numerator)
      tibble::tibble(
        factor = contrast_factor_col,
        group_1 = numerator,
        group_2 = paste0("Non", numerator),
        contrast_expr = paste0(numerator, " - (", paste0(others, collapse = " + "), ")/", length(others))
      )
    })
    return(dplyr::distinct(rows))
  }

  rows <- purrr::pmap_dfr(spec, function(factor, numerator, denominator) {
    numerator <- validate_contrast_level(numerator, "Numerator", levs, contrast_factor_col)
    denominator <- validate_contrast_level(denominator, "Denominator", levs, contrast_factor_col)
    tibble::tibble(
      factor = contrast_factor_col,
      group_1 = numerator,
      group_2 = denominator,
      contrast_expr = paste0(numerator, "-", denominator)
    )
  })

  dplyr::distinct(rows)
}


# Assemble all pre-model inputs in one place: metadata preparation,
# low-power diagnostics, shared-group detection, and contrast-table creation.
build_analysis_bundle <- function(
  geodiff_obj = NULL,
  q3_obj,
  geodiff_norm_obj = NULL,
  tmm_obj,
  contrast_file_path,
  contrast_factor_col = contrast_factor,
  model_group = model_group_col,
  model_mode = "one_vs_rest_mixed",
  min_samples_per_group = 2,
  min_patients_per_group = "",
  auto_exclude_low_power = FALSE
) {
  if (is.null(q3_obj)) {
    stop("Q3/negative-probe normalised object is required for stage 5 DE calculation.", call. = FALSE)
  }
  if (is.null(tmm_obj)) {
    stop("TMM normalised object is required for stage 5 DE calculation.", call. = FALSE)
  }

  if (!is.null(geodiff_obj)) {
    geodiff_obj <- prepare_metadata(geodiff_obj, contrast_factor_col = contrast_factor_col, model_group = model_group)
  }
  q3_obj <- prepare_metadata(q3_obj, contrast_factor_col = contrast_factor_col, model_group = model_group)
  if (!is.null(geodiff_norm_obj)) {
    geodiff_norm_obj <- prepare_metadata(geodiff_norm_obj, contrast_factor_col = contrast_factor_col, model_group = model_group)
  }
  tmm_obj <- prepare_metadata(tmm_obj, contrast_factor_col = contrast_factor_col, model_group = model_group)

  bundle_objects <- list(
    q3 = q3_obj,
    tmm = tmm_obj,
    geodiff = geodiff_obj,
    geodiff_norm = geodiff_norm_obj
  )
  available_objects <- bundle_objects[!vapply(bundle_objects, is.null, logical(1))]

  power_diag <- find_low_power_groups(
    get_pheno(q3_obj),
    group_col = model_group,
    min_samples_per_group = min_samples_per_group,
    min_patients_per_group = min_patients_per_group,
    patient_col = col_patient
  )

  low_power_groups <- power_diag[[model_group]][power_diag$low_power]
  if (length(low_power_groups) > 0) {
    warning("Low-power groups detected: ", paste(low_power_groups, collapse = ", "))
    if (isTRUE(auto_exclude_low_power)) {
      available_objects <- lapply(
        available_objects,
        remove_low_power_groups,
        low_power_groups = low_power_groups,
        group_col = model_group
      )
      q3_obj <- available_objects$q3
      tmm_obj <- available_objects$tmm
      geodiff_obj <- available_objects$geodiff
      geodiff_norm_obj <- available_objects$geodiff_norm
    }
  }

  available_objects <- list(
    q3 = q3_obj,
    tmm = tmm_obj,
    geodiff = geodiff_obj,
    geodiff_norm = geodiff_norm_obj
  )
  available_objects <- available_objects[!vapply(available_objects, is.null, logical(1))]

  shared_levels <- Reduce(intersect, lapply(
    available_objects,
    function(obj) eligible_group_levels(get_pheno(obj), group_col = model_group, min_group_n = min_samples_per_group)
  ))

  if (length(shared_levels) < 2) {
    stop(
      sprintf(
        "Need at least two shared DE groups across available inputs for contrast_factor '%s'. Available objects: %s. Shared levels detected: %s.",
        contrast_factor_col,
        paste(names(available_objects), collapse = ", "),
        ifelse(length(shared_levels) == 0, "none", paste(shared_levels, collapse = ", "))
      ),
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }

  contrast_table <- read_contrast_spec(
    path_to_file = contrast_file_path,
    group_levels = shared_levels,
    model_mode = model_mode,
    contrast_factor_col = contrast_factor_col
  )

  list(
    geodiff = geodiff_obj,
    q3 = q3_obj,
    geodiff_norm = geodiff_norm_obj,
    tmm = tmm_obj,
    available_object_names = names(available_objects),
    shared_levels = shared_levels,
    contrast_factor = contrast_factor_col,
    model_group_col = model_group,
    contrast_table = contrast_table,
    power_diagnostics = power_diag
  )
}


# Write object/group/contrast diagnostics so users can audit what entered model fitting.
emit_parity_diagnostics <- function(bundle, out_tables_dir, random_effect_var, contrast_file_path, contrast_factor_col, model_group_col) {
  bundle_objects <- list(
    geodiff = bundle$geodiff,
    q3 = bundle$q3,
    geodiff_norm = bundle$geodiff_norm,
    tmm = bundle$tmm
  )
  available_objects <- bundle_objects[!vapply(bundle_objects, is.null, logical(1))]

  # Object-level diagnostics
  object_diag <- purrr::imap_dfr(available_objects, function(obj, object_name) {
    tibble::tibble(
      object_name = object_name,
      object_class = paste(class(obj), collapse = ", "),
      n_features = nrow(get_expr_mat(obj)),
      n_samples = ncol(get_expr_mat(obj))
    )
  })

  # Annotation-level diagnostics
  ann_diag <- purrr::imap_dfr(available_objects, function(obj, object_name) {
    get_pheno(obj) %>%
      dplyr::count(Annotation, name = "n") %>%
      dplyr::mutate(object_name = object_name)
  }) %>% dplyr::arrange(object_name, dplyr::desc(n), Annotation)

  count_de_groups <- function(obj, object_name) {
    get_pheno(obj) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(DE_Group = .data[[model_group_col]]) %>%
      dplyr::count(DE_Group, name = "n") %>%
      dplyr::mutate(object_name = object_name)
  }

  group_diag <- purrr::imap_dfr(available_objects, function(obj, object_name) {
    count_de_groups(obj, object_name)
  }) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(contrast_factor = contrast_factor_col) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(object_name, dplyr::desc(n), DE_Group)

  shared_diag <- tibble::tibble(
    contrast_factor = contrast_factor_col,
    shared_de_group_level = bundle$shared_levels
  )
  contrast_diag <- bundle$contrast_table

  readr::write_csv(object_diag, path(out_tables_dir, "parity_diagnostics_object_summary.csv"))
  readr::write_csv(ann_diag, path(out_tables_dir, "parity_diagnostics_annotation_counts.csv"))
  readr::write_csv(group_diag, path(out_tables_dir, "parity_diagnostics_de_group_counts.csv"))
  readr::write_csv(shared_diag, path(out_tables_dir, "parity_diagnostics_shared_levels.csv"))
  readr::write_csv(contrast_diag, path(out_tables_dir, "parity_diagnostics_contrast_table.csv"))

  message("Consistency diagnostics written to: ", out_tables_dir)
  message("Contrast file in use: ", contrast_file_path)
  message("Contrast factor in use: ", contrast_factor_col, " (modeled internally as ", model_group_col, ")")
  message("Random intercept variable: ", random_effect_var)
  message("Shared DE group levels: ", paste(bundle$shared_levels, collapse = ", "))
}

# Generic cache utility for method result objects.
# This is intentionally reusable across limma branches so behaviour is consistent.
#
# Cache policy in this protocol:
# - If use_cache = TRUE and file exists:
#     - load RDS
#     - accept only non-empty list objects
#     - otherwise warn and recompute
# - If use_cache = FALSE or cache missing/invalid:
#     - run `fit_fn()`
#     - save result to cache_path
run_or_load_method_cache <- function(cache_path, use_cache, method_label, fit_fn,
                                     expected_contrasts = NULL) {
  if (isTRUE(use_cache) && file_exists(cache_path)) {
    message("[", method_label, "] Cache enabled. Found cache file: ", cache_path)
    cached <- readRDS(cache_path)
    # Accept the cache only when it is a non-empty list AND, when contrast names are
    # known, its names match the current contrast expressions. This prevents loading
    # stale results after contrast_file/model_mode/contrast_factor changes.
    cache_valid <- is.list(cached) && length(cached) > 0 &&
      (is.null(expected_contrasts) || identical(names(cached), expected_contrasts))
    if (cache_valid) {
      message("[", method_label, "] Loaded cached results successfully.")
      return(cached)
    }
    warning("[", method_label, "] Cache file exists but is invalid/empty or does not match the current contrast expressions. Recomputing and overwriting cache.")
  } else if (isTRUE(use_cache)) {
    message("[", method_label, "] Cache enabled, but no cache file found. Fitting model now.")
  } else {
    message("[", method_label, "] Cache disabled. Fitting model now.")
  }

  out <- fit_fn()
  saveRDS(out, cache_path)
  message("[", method_label, "] Saved results cache to: ", cache_path)
  out
}

resolve_model_cache_setting <- function(use_cache, method_label, covariate_spec) {
  if (isTRUE(use_cache) && length(model_covariate_names(covariate_spec)) > 0) {
    warning(
      "[", method_label, "] Cache disabled because fixed-effect covariates are requested. ",
      "Refitting ensures the current covariate formula is applied.",
      call. = FALSE
    )
    return(FALSE)
  }
  isTRUE(use_cache)
}

# Save a base-graphics DE diagnostic (a voom/voomWithDreamWeights mean-variance
# trend, or a limma-trend plotSA) to a deterministic per-branch PNG under the
# stage-5 figure directory. The deterministic name (meanvar_<branch>.png) lets the
# rendering section locate the figure later by reconstructing the same path, and
# means a cached fit (which never reaches this code) simply leaves any earlier PNG
# untouched. Drawing errors are downgraded to a warning and return NA so a single
# failed diagnostic never aborts model fitting.
save_de_diagnostic_png <- function(branch_label, draw_fn, out_dir = out_figures_de,
                                   width = 1400, height = 1100, res = 150) {
  if (is.null(branch_label) || !nzchar(as.character(branch_label))) return(NA_character_)
  file_name <- paste0("meanvar_", sanitize_col_name(branch_label, "branch"), ".png")
  png_path <- protocol_contract_path(
    out_dir, file_name, portable_path_limit,
    paste0("Stage 5 mean-variance diagnostic (", branch_label, ")")
  )
  ok <- FALSE
  grDevices::png(filename = png_path, width = width, height = height, res = res)
  tryCatch(
    {
      draw_fn()
      ok <- TRUE
    },
    error = function(e) {
      warning("[", branch_label, "] Could not render mean-variance diagnostic: ",
              conditionMessage(e), call. = FALSE)
    },
    finally = grDevices::dev.off()
  )
  if (isTRUE(ok)) png_path else NA_character_
}

# limma-voom branch for count-like data with duplicateCorrelation where possible.
run_limma_voom <- function(obj, contrastTable, random_effect_var, model_covariate_spec,
                           branch_label = NULL) {
  pd <- get_pheno(obj)
  pd <- prepare_model_metadata(pd, model_covariate_spec)
  validate_model_covariates(pd, model_covariate_spec)

  # voom workflow starts from count-like matrices and estimates mean-variance weights.
  # TMM scaling is represented by calcNormFactors(d); voom therefore leaves
  # between-array normalisation disabled rather than applying an additional
  # quantile normalisation step.
  counts_mat <- clean_count_matrix(
    get_assay_matrix(obj, assay_name = if (inherits(obj, "DGEList")) "counts" else "exprs", context = "limma-voom count input"),
    context = "limma-voom count input"
  )
  d <- DGEList(counts = counts_mat, group = pd[[model_group_col]])
  d <- calcNormFactors(d)

  design_info <- build_limma_design(pd, model_covariate_spec, group_col = model_group_col)
  design <- design_info$design

  # First voom pass gets initial precision weights. If a random-effect column is available,
  # duplicateCorrelation is used to account for repeated sampling within patient/slide.
  block_var <- resolve_random_effect_var(pd, random_effect_var)
  v <- voom(d, design = design, normalize.method = "none", plot = FALSE)

  if (!is.na(block_var)) {
    corfit <- duplicateCorrelation(v, design = design, block = pd[[block_var]])
    v <- voom(d, design = design, normalize.method = "none", plot = FALSE,
              block = pd[[block_var]], correlation = corfit$consensus)
    corfit <- duplicateCorrelation(v, design = design, block = pd[[block_var]])
    fit <- lmFit(v, design, block = pd[[block_var]], correlation = corfit$consensus)
  } else {
    fit <- lmFit(v, design)
  }

  # Mean-variance diagnostic: the standard un-blocked voom trend (matches the voom
  # vignette QC view). Captured here because the fitted objects only exist during
  # fitting; a cached run skips this and reuses any existing PNG.
  save_de_diagnostic_png(branch_label, function() {
    voom(d, design = design, normalize.method = "none", plot = TRUE)
    graphics::title(sub = paste0("limma-voom mean-variance trend: ", branch_label))
  })

  coef_levels <- colnames(coef(fit))
  validate_contrast_symbols(contrastTable$contrast_expr, coef_levels, context = "limma-voom")
  cm <- makeContrasts(contrasts = contrastTable$contrast_expr, levels = coef_levels)
  fit2 <- eBayes(contrasts.fit(fit, cm), trend = FALSE, robust = TRUE)

  res <- lapply(seq_len(ncol(cm)), function(i) getResultsDataFrame(fit2, i, contrastTable$group_1[i], contrastTable$group_2[i]))
  names(res) <- colnames(cm)
  res
}

# limma-trend branch for transformed/normalised expression matrices.
run_limma_trend <- function(
  obj,
  contrastTable,
  random_effect_var,
  model_covariate_spec,
  assay_name = "exprs",
  transform = "identity",
  prior_count = 0,
  input_label = "limma-trend input",
  branch_label = NULL
) {
  pd <- get_pheno(obj)
  pd <- prepare_model_metadata(pd, model_covariate_spec)
  validate_model_covariates(pd, model_covariate_spec)
  # limma-trend branch operates directly on transformed/normalised expression.
  design_info <- build_limma_design(pd, model_covariate_spec, group_col = model_group_col)
  design <- design_info$design

  # duplicateCorrelation gives random-intercept-like handling in limma when possible.
  expr_mat <- prepare_model_expression_matrix(
    obj,
    assay_name = assay_name,
    transform = transform,
    prior_count = prior_count,
    context = input_label,
    clean_na = TRUE
  )
  block_var <- resolve_random_effect_var(pd, random_effect_var)

  if (!is.na(block_var)) {
    corfit <- duplicateCorrelation(expr_mat, design = design, block = pd[[block_var]])
    fit <- lmFit(expr_mat, design = design, block = pd[[block_var]], correlation = corfit$consensus)
  } else {
    fit <- lmFit(expr_mat, design)
  }

  coef_levels <- colnames(coef(fit))
  validate_contrast_symbols(contrastTable$contrast_expr, coef_levels, context = "limma-trend")
  cm <- makeContrasts(contrasts = contrastTable$contrast_expr, levels = coef_levels)
  fit2 <- eBayes(contrasts.fit(fit, cm), trend = TRUE, robust = TRUE)

  # Mean-variance diagnostic for limma-trend: plotSA shows each gene's residual SD
  # against average log-expression with the empirical-Bayes trend line.
  save_de_diagnostic_png(branch_label, function()
    limma::plotSA(fit2, main = paste0("limma-trend mean-variance (plotSA): ", branch_label)))

  res <- lapply(seq_len(ncol(cm)), function(i) getResultsDataFrame(fit2, i, contrastTable$group_1[i], contrastTable$group_2[i]))
  names(res) <- colnames(cm)
  res
}

translate_contrasts_for_dream <- function(contrast_expr, coefficient_map) {
  escape_regex <- function(x) {
    gsub("([][{}()+*^$|\\\\?.])", "\\\\\\1", x, perl = TRUE)
  }

  out <- as.character(contrast_expr)
  for (symbol in names(coefficient_map)[order(nchar(names(coefficient_map)), decreasing = TRUE)]) {
    pattern <- paste0(
      "(?<![A-Za-z0-9_.])",
      escape_regex(symbol),
      "(?![A-Za-z0-9_.])"
    )
    out <- gsub(pattern, coefficient_map[[symbol]], out, perl = TRUE)
  }
  out
}

# dream branch for mixed-model DE; fits one global group model and applies the
# same expanded contrast expressions used by limma.
run_dream <- function(
  obj,
  contrastTable,
  random_effect_var,
  cache_path,
  model_covariate_spec,
  use_cache = TRUE,
  allow_random_slope = FALSE,
  seed = NULL,
  branch_label = NULL
) {
  expected_contrasts <- as.character(contrastTable$contrast_expr)
  requested_covariates <- model_covariate_names(model_covariate_spec)

  if (isTRUE(use_cache) && length(requested_covariates) > 0) {
    warning(
      "Dream cache disabled for this run because fixed-effect covariates are requested. ",
      "Refitting ensures the current covariate formula is applied.",
      call. = FALSE
    )
    use_cache <- FALSE
  }

  if (use_cache && file_exists(cache_path)) {
    cached <- readRDS(cache_path)
    if (
      is.list(cached) &&
      length(cached) > 0 &&
      identical(names(cached), expected_contrasts)
    ) {
      return(cached)
    }
    warning("dream cache exists but is invalid or does not match the current expanded contrast expressions; refitting and overwriting cache.")
  }

  pd <- get_pheno(obj)
  if (!(model_group_col %in% colnames(pd))) {
    stop("Model group column not found in metadata for dream: ", model_group_col, call. = FALSE)
  }

  pd[[model_group_col]] <- factor(
    as.character(pd[[model_group_col]]),
    levels = sort(unique(as.character(pd[[model_group_col]])))
  )
  if (length(levels(pd[[model_group_col]])) < 2) {
    warning("Dream skipped because fewer than two DE groups were available.")
    return(list())
  }

  pd <- prepare_model_metadata(pd, model_covariate_spec)
  validate_model_covariates(pd, model_covariate_spec)

  block_var <- resolve_random_effect_var(pd, random_effect_var)
  if (is.na(block_var) || length(unique(pd[[block_var]])) < 2) {
    warning("Dream skipped because no valid random-effect column with >=2 levels was available.")
    return(list())
  }

  counts_mat <- clean_count_matrix(
    get_assay_matrix(obj, assay_name = if (inherits(obj, "DGEList")) "counts" else "exprs", context = "dream count input"),
    context = "dream count input"
  )
  y <- edgeR::DGEList(counts = counts_mat)
  y <- edgeR::calcNormFactors(y)

  workers <- max(1, parallel::detectCores() - 1)
  # Seed the parallel backend so dream's stochastic steps are reproducible: the
  # top-level set.seed() does not propagate to BiocParallel workers.
  bp <- if (.Platform$OS.type == "windows") {
    BiocParallel::SnowParam(workers = workers, RNGseed = seed)
  } else {
    BiocParallel::MulticoreParam(workers = workers, RNGseed = seed)
  }

  fixed_formula_text <- build_fixed_effect_formula_text(model_covariate_spec, group_col = model_group_col)
  fixed_design <- stats::model.matrix(
    stats::as.formula(fixed_formula_text),
    data = pd
  )
  if (nrow(fixed_design) != nrow(pd)) {
    stop(
      "The dream fixed-effect model matrix has fewer rows than the prepared metadata. ",
      "Check for missing values in model covariates or the contrast factor before fitting.",
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }
  validate_design_matrix(fixed_design, context = "dream fixed-effect design")

  coefficient_names <- colnames(fixed_design)
  group_prefix <- paste0("^", make.names(model_group_col))
  group_coefficient_names <- coefficient_names[grepl(group_prefix, coefficient_names)]
  coefficient_map <- stats::setNames(
    group_coefficient_names,
    sub(group_prefix, "", group_coefficient_names)
  )

  validate_contrast_symbols(
    contrastTable$contrast_expr,
    names(coefficient_map),
    context = "dream"
  )
  dream_contrasts <- translate_contrasts_for_dream(
    contrastTable$contrast_expr,
    coefficient_map
  )

  fixed_rhs_text <- paste(
    vapply(c(model_group_col, requested_covariates), quote_formula_term, character(1)),
    collapse = " + "
  )
  form_intercept <- stats::as.formula(paste0("~ 0 + ", fixed_rhs_text, " + (1|`", block_var, "`)"))
  form_slope <- stats::as.formula(paste0("~ 0 + ", fixed_rhs_text, " + (1 + ", quote_formula_term(model_group_col), "|`", block_var, "`)"))
  message("Dream fixed-effect formula: ", fixed_formula_text)

  fit <- tryCatch({
    form_use <- if (isTRUE(allow_random_slope)) form_slope else form_intercept
    vobj <- variancePartition::voomWithDreamWeights(y, form_use, pd, BPPARAM = bp)
    L <- variancePartition::makeContrastsDream(form_use, pd, contrasts = dream_contrasts)
    fit_i <- variancePartition::dream(vobj, form_use, pd, L)
    variancePartition::eBayes(fit_i)
  }, error = function(e) {
    if (isTRUE(allow_random_slope)) {
      warning("Dream random-slope global model failed; falling back to random-intercept.")
      vobj <- variancePartition::voomWithDreamWeights(y, form_intercept, pd, BPPARAM = bp)
      L <- variancePartition::makeContrastsDream(form_intercept, pd, contrasts = dream_contrasts)
      fit_i <- variancePartition::dream(vobj, form_intercept, pd, L)
      return(variancePartition::eBayes(fit_i))
    }
    stop("Dream failed for global contrast model: ", conditionMessage(e), call. = FALSE)
  })

  # Mean-variance diagnostic for dream. voomWithDreamWeights draws the same kind of
  # trend as limma-voom; it is recomputed once with the random-intercept form (always
  # valid, representative trend shape) purely to capture the figure, keeping device
  # handling isolated from the slope/intercept fallback above.
  save_de_diagnostic_png(branch_label, function() {
    variancePartition::voomWithDreamWeights(y, form_intercept, pd, BPPARAM = bp, plot = TRUE)
    graphics::title(sub = paste0("dream mean-variance trend (random intercept): ", branch_label))
  })

  out <- lapply(seq_along(expected_contrasts), function(i) {
    getResultsDataFrame(fit, i, contrastTable$group_1[i], contrastTable$group_2[i])
  })
  names(out) <- expected_contrasts

  if (length(out) == 0) {
    warning("Dream did not return any valid contrast results.")
    return(list())
  }

  saveRDS(out, cache_path)
  out
}


# Clean result tables before plotting to avoid NA-driven plotting failures.
sanitize_results_table <- function(results_table, method_label = "method") {
  cleaned <- lapply(seq_along(results_table), function(i) {
    nm <- names(results_table)[i]
    df <- as.data.frame(results_table[[i]])

    if (!all(c("logFC", "adj.P.Val") %in% colnames(df))) {
      return(NULL)
    }

    keep <- !is.na(df$logFC) & !is.na(df$adj.P.Val)
    dropped <- sum(!keep)
    if (dropped > 0) {
      message("[", method_label, ":", nm, "] dropping ", dropped, " rows with NA logFC/adj.P.Val before plotting.")
    }
    df <- df[keep, , drop = FALSE]
    if (nrow(df) == 0) return(NULL)
    df
  })

  names(cleaned) <- names(results_table)
  cleaned <- cleaned[!vapply(cleaned, is.null, logical(1))]
  cleaned
}

# Build Excel-safe, unique worksheet names from contrast names.
# Excel sheet-name rules: <= 31 characters; cannot contain [ ] : * ? / \ ;
# cannot be blank; no leading/trailing apostrophe; must be unique (Excel compares
# names case-insensitively). The reserved "_index" key sheet is seeded into the
# "seen" set so no contrast can ever collide with it. Full contrast names are
# preserved separately in the _index sheet, so truncation here is lossless overall.
make_excel_sheet_names <- function(contrast_names) {
  seen <- tolower("_index")
  vapply(as.character(contrast_names), function(nm) {
    # perl = TRUE so backslash escaping works inside the class; the default TRE
    # engine treats "\" as literal and lets "]" close the class early, which would
    # leave Excel-illegal characters in the sheet name.
    base <- gsub("[\\\\/?*\\[\\]:]", "_", nm, perl = TRUE)
    base <- gsub("^'+|'+$", "", base)
    base <- trimws(base)
    if (!nzchar(base)) base <- "contrast"
    base <- substr(base, 1, 31)
    candidate <- base
    suffix <- 1L
    while (tolower(candidate) %in% seen) {
      suffix <- suffix + 1L
      tag <- paste0("_", suffix)
      candidate <- paste0(substr(base, 1, max(0L, 31L - nchar(tag))), tag)
    }
    seen[[length(seen) + 1L]] <<- tolower(candidate)
    candidate
  }, character(1), USE.NAMES = FALSE)
}

# Write one workbook with a worksheet per contrast plus an "_index" key sheet that
# maps the (possibly truncated) sheet name back to the full contrast name. When
# significant_only = TRUE each sheet is filtered to genes passing the adj.P/logFC
# thresholds; contrasts with no significant genes keep a header-only sheet so the
# workbook structure stays stable. write_xlsx failures (e.g. the file is open in
# Excel on Windows) are downgraded to a warning rather than aborting the stage.
write_contrast_excel <- function(results_table, excel_path, method_label, significant_only = FALSE) {
  contrast_names <- names(results_table)
  sheet_names <- make_excel_sheet_names(contrast_names)

  sheets <- lapply(seq_along(results_table), function(i) {
    df <- as.data.frame(results_table[[i]])
    df <- cbind(gene_name = rownames(df), df, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
    rownames(df) <- NULL
    if (isTRUE(significant_only)) {
      keep <- !is.na(df$adj.P.Val) & !is.na(df$logFC) &
        df$adj.P.Val < params$adj_p_cutoff & abs(df$logFC) > params$logFC_cutoff
      df <- df[keep, , drop = FALSE]
    }
    df
  })
  names(sheets) <- sheet_names

  index_sheet <- tibble::tibble(
    sheet_name = sheet_names,
    contrast = as.character(contrast_names),
    n_genes = vapply(sheets, nrow, integer(1))
  )

  workbook <- c(list(`_index` = index_sheet), sheets)

  tryCatch(
    {
      writexl::write_xlsx(workbook, path = excel_path)
      message("[", method_label, "] Excel export written: ", excel_path)
    },
    error = function(e) {
      warning(
        "[", method_label, "] Could not write Excel workbook '", excel_path, "': ",
        conditionMessage(e), ". If the file is open in Excel, close it and re-run.",
        call. = FALSE
      )
    }
  )
  invisible(excel_path)
}

# Save method outputs (tables + DE statistics + gene lists) in a consistent file layout.
# In stage 5 we intentionally avoid manuscript-style plotting; figures are produced in stage 6.
save_method_outputs <- function(results_table, method_label, data_for_plots = NULL) {
  results_table <- sanitize_results_table(results_table, method_label = method_label)
  if (length(results_table) == 0) {
    warning("No valid results to save for ", method_label, " after NA filtering.")
    return(invisible(list(summary = data.frame(), de_genes = character(0), stats = data.frame())))
  }

  summary_df <- purrr::imap_dfr(results_table, function(tbl, contrast_name) {
    tbl <- as.data.frame(tbl)
    sig_tbl <- tbl[tbl$adj.P.Val < params$adj_p_cutoff & abs(tbl$logFC) > params$logFC_cutoff, , drop = FALSE]
    tibble::tibble(
      method = method_label,
      contrast = contrast_name,
      n_tested = nrow(tbl),
      n_sig = nrow(sig_tbl),
      n_up = sum(sig_tbl$logFC > 0, na.rm = TRUE),
      n_down = sum(sig_tbl$logFC < 0, na.rm = TRUE)
    )
  })

  readr::write_csv(summary_df, path(out_results_de, paste0(method_label, "_res_summary.csv")))
  saveRDS(results_table, file = path(out_results_de, paste0(method_label, "_results.RDS")))

  # Per-contrast Excel exports so users can browse results or send them to a
  # collaborator without importing the RDS into R. One worksheet per contrast plus
  # an _index key sheet. The full workbook keeps every tested gene; the
  # significant-only workbook keeps genes passing the adj.P/logFC thresholds.
  write_contrast_excel(
    results_table, path(out_results_de, paste0(method_label, "_results.xlsx")),
    method_label, significant_only = FALSE
  )
  write_contrast_excel(
    results_table, path(out_results_de, paste0(method_label, "_results_significant.xlsx")),
    method_label, significant_only = TRUE
  )

  # Carry each contrast's gene names from its own rownames before binding, rather
  # than recovering them from rbind-mangled "<contrast>.<gene>" rownames with a
  # greedy gsub (which truncated any gene/target name containing a dot, e.g.
  # "HLA.DRB1" -> "DRB1").
  de_genes <- dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(results_table, function(df) {
    df$gene_name <- rownames(df)
    df
  }))
  de_genes <- unique(de_genes[de_genes$adj.P.Val < params$adj_p_cutoff & abs(de_genes$logFC) > params$logFC_cutoff, ]$gene_name)
  saveRDS(de_genes, file = path(out_results_de, paste0(method_label, "_DE_genes.RDS")))

  method_stats <- tibble::tibble(
    method = method_label,
    n_contrasts = length(results_table),
    n_genes_passing_threshold = length(de_genes),
    adj_p_cutoff = params$adj_p_cutoff,
    logFC_cutoff = params$logFC_cutoff
  )
  readr::write_csv(method_stats, path(out_results_de, paste0(method_label, "_method_stats.csv")))

  message(
    sprintf(
      "[%s] completed successfully: %d contrasts processed; %d genes pass adj.P.Val < %.3f and |logFC| > %.3f.",
      method_label,
      method_stats$n_contrasts,
      method_stats$n_genes_passing_threshold,
      params$adj_p_cutoff,
      params$logFC_cutoff
    )
  )

  invisible(list(summary = summary_df, de_genes = de_genes, stats = method_stats))
}

model_input_audit_row <- function(
  branch,
  obj,
  assay_name,
  transform = "identity",
  transform_label = "none",
  prior_count = 0,
  matrix_summarised = "model matrix",
  input_role = ""
) {
  raw_mat <- prepare_model_expression_matrix(
    obj,
    assay_name = assay_name,
    transform = transform,
    prior_count = prior_count,
    context = paste(branch, "audit input"),
    clean_na = FALSE
  )
  model_mat <- drop_rows_with_na(raw_mat, context = paste(branch, "audit input"))
  finite_values <- as.numeric(model_mat[is.finite(model_mat)])

  tibble::tibble(
    branch = branch,
    object_class = paste(class(obj), collapse = ", "),
    assay_used = assay_name,
    transformation_applied = transform_label,
    matrix_summarised = matrix_summarised,
    dimensions = paste(nrow(model_mat), ncol(model_mat), sep = " x "),
    min = if (length(finite_values) > 0) round(min(finite_values), 4) else NA_real_,
    median = if (length(finite_values) > 0) round(stats::median(finite_values), 4) else NA_real_,
    max = if (length(finite_values) > 0) round(max(finite_values), 4) else NA_real_,
    integer_like = matrix_is_integer_like(model_mat),
    na_count = sum(is.na(raw_mat)),
    rows_removed_for_na = nrow(raw_mat) - nrow(model_mat),
    input_role = input_role
  )
}

model_covariate_type <- function(covariate_name, covariate_spec) {
  if (covariate_name %in% covariate_spec$continuous) return("continuous_numeric")
  if (covariate_name %in% covariate_spec$categorical) return("categorical_factor")
  "not_requested"
}

count_numeric_parse_failures <- function(x) {
  raw_chr <- trimws(as.character(x))
  raw_missing <- is.na(x) | is.na(raw_chr) | !nzchar(raw_chr)
  parsed <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(raw_chr))
  sum(!raw_missing & is.na(parsed))
}

model_covariate_audit_rows <- function(obj, object_name, covariate_spec, group_col = model_group_col) {
  pd_raw <- get_pheno(obj)
  pd_model <- prepare_model_metadata(pd_raw, covariate_spec)
  validate_model_covariates(pd_model, covariate_spec)
  design_info <- build_limma_design(pd_model, covariate_spec, group_col = group_col)
  design <- design_info$design
  covariates <- model_covariate_names(covariate_spec)

  if (length(covariates) == 0) {
    return(tibble::tibble(
      object_name = object_name,
      formula_text = design_info$formula_text,
      covariate = "None",
      requested_type = "none",
      imported_class = NA_character_,
      model_class = NA_character_,
      n_samples = nrow(pd_model),
      n_missing_after_preparation = NA_integer_,
      n_distinct_observed = NA_integer_,
      numeric_parse_failures = NA_integer_,
      design_columns = paste(colnames(design), collapse = ", "),
      model_role = "No additional fixed-effect covariates requested; model includes the DE group only.",
      interpretation = "Default fixed-effect design. Review the DE group columns and contrast table before fitting."
    ))
  }

  design_assign <- attr(design, "assign")
  purrr::map_dfr(seq_along(covariates), function(i) {
    cov <- covariates[[i]]
    requested_type <- model_covariate_type(cov, covariate_spec)
    term_index <- i + 1
    encoded_columns <- colnames(design)[design_assign == term_index]
    is_continuous <- identical(requested_type, "continuous_numeric")

    tibble::tibble(
      object_name = object_name,
      formula_text = design_info$formula_text,
      covariate = cov,
      requested_type = requested_type,
      imported_class = paste(class(pd_raw[[cov]]), collapse = ", "),
      model_class = paste(class(pd_model[[cov]]), collapse = ", "),
      n_samples = nrow(pd_model),
      n_missing_after_preparation = sum(is.na(pd_model[[cov]])),
      n_distinct_observed = dplyr::n_distinct(stats::na.omit(pd_model[[cov]])),
      numeric_parse_failures = if (is_continuous) count_numeric_parse_failures(pd_raw[[cov]]) else NA_integer_,
      design_columns = paste(encoded_columns, collapse = ", "),
      model_role = if (is_continuous) {
        "Continuous adjustment term; expected to contribute one numeric design column."
      } else {
        "Categorical adjustment term; expected to contribute factor-expanded design column(s)."
      },
      interpretation = if (is_continuous) {
        "Confirm that this is a measured numeric covariate, such as age, and not a coded group label."
      } else {
        "Confirm that these levels represent intended adjustment groups and are not continuous measurements stored as text."
      }
    )
  })
}

This section loads the inputs, prepares metadata, and writes diagnostics before any model fitting starts. If execution fails here, inspect the generated path settings, metadata column names, and low-power diagnostics first.

Show code 
# input loading can emit expected class/version warnings from serialized objects.
#| warning: false

# Load all expression objects needed for method/normalisation comparisons.
geodiff_input_exists <- file_exists(geodiff_input_path)
norm_geodiff_input_exists <- file_exists(norm_geodiff_input_path)

de_input_status <- tibble::tibble(
  Input = c("GeoDiff-filtered count object", "Q3/negative-probe normalised object", "GeoDiff-normalized object", "TMM normalised object"),
  Required_for_non_geodiff_de = c("No", "Yes", "No", "Yes"),
  Path = c(geodiff_input_path, norm_input_path, norm_geodiff_input_path, norm_tmm_input_path),
  File_found = c(geodiff_input_exists, file_exists(norm_input_path), norm_geodiff_input_exists, file_exists(norm_tmm_input_path)),
  Action = c(
    ifelse(geodiff_input_exists, "Available for dream_GeoDiffCounts and limma_voom_GeoDiff as GeoDiff-filtered count data.", "GeoDiff-filtered count-data DE branches will be skipped."),
    "Used for limma_trend_Q3 and shared contrast setup. Stage 5 explicitly extracts the q_norm assay for modeling.",
    ifelse(norm_geodiff_input_exists, "Available for limma_trend_GeoDiff and GeoDiff-normalized reference outputs; not used as voom count input.", "GeoDiff-normalized limma-trend branch will be skipped."),
    "Used for limma_voom_TMM and shared contrast setup."
  )
)

render_protocol_kable(
  de_input_status,
  caption = "Stage 5 input file availability. GeoDiff files are optional; when absent, GeoDiff-only DE branches are skipped and non-GeoDiff branches continue."
)
Stage 5 input file availability. GeoDiff files are optional; when absent, GeoDiff-only DE branches are skipped and non-GeoDiff branches continue.
Input Required_for_non_geodiff_de Path File_found Action
GeoDiff-filtered count object No C:/R/gmx/Example_Dataset/Outputs/2_GeoDiff/results/GeoDiff_spatial_data.RDS TRUE Available for dream_GeoDiffCounts and limma_voom_GeoDiff as GeoDiff-filtered count data.
Q3/negative-probe normalised object Yes C:/R/gmx/Example_Dataset/Outputs/3_Norm/results/normalised_spatial_data.RDS TRUE Used for limma_trend_Q3 and shared contrast setup. Stage 5 explicitly extracts the q_norm assay for modeling.
GeoDiff-normalized object No C:/R/gmx/Example_Dataset/Outputs/3_Norm/results/GeoDiff_normalised_spatial_data.RDS TRUE Available for limma_trend_GeoDiff and GeoDiff-normalized reference outputs; not used as voom count input.
TMM normalised object Yes C:/R/gmx/Example_Dataset/Outputs/3_Norm/results/TMM_normalised_spatial_data.RDS TRUE Used for limma_voom_TMM and shared contrast setup.
Show code 
if (!geodiff_input_exists || !norm_geodiff_input_exists) {
  missing_geodiff_inputs <- c(
    if (!geodiff_input_exists) paste0("GeoDiff-filtered count data: ", geodiff_input_path),
